dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a report of Assault/Rape at Shell gas station near West Broad and 28th.
Hi, I'm Derek, an EMT with the rescue squad. What happened to make you call 911?
I was punched in the face. I have a headache and pain on the right side of my face.
Can you tell me your name and where you are now?
Dan.
Are you inside a hospital or outside somewhere?
I'm on the sidewalk near the gas station.
We're going to take a quick look at your airway, okay?
How are you feeling when you breathe in and out?
Your breathing is non-labored with a rate of 15 breaths per minute and adequate depth.
Your pulse is strong and regular.
Your skin is normal in color and warm. There's blood on your face, and the bleeding has stopped.
Your neck is tender to the touch. Per protocol (Injury - General Trauma Management, Protocol 4-1), manual stabilization will be performed to protect your spine.
There is swelling and a small laceration on your lip. No active bleeding.
There is no back pain or deformity noted.
We'll now take your blood pressure.
Your pulse is 101 beats per minute.
Your breathing is 20 times per minute.
Blood sugar is at 172.
Oxygen saturation is 97%.
Applying the ECG leads now for heart monitoring.
Your blood pressure is 185 over 122.
To stabilize your cervical spine, I'll manually hold it in position while you are lying down.
I will now apply an X-Collar to support your neck. This may feel tight, but it's for protection.
I am applying a cold pack to your right cheek and lip to reduce the swelling.
Placing you on a 4-lead ECG monitor now for heart rhythm.
Can you describe the type of pain you're feeling?
You have a history of non-insulin dependent diabetes. How are you managing it?
We were in the store when someone came in screaming about a man having been beaten up. That's all we know.
What was the last thing you had to eat or drink?
Do you have a history of seasonal allergies that require medications or tests?
I've noticed a scent of alcohol on your breath. Have you had something to drink today?
It sounds like you're experiencing severe pain.
Pulse is now 96.
Respiration is 15 and non-labored.
Your blood pressure is slightly increased at 188 over 125.
Oxygen saturation is still at 97%.
EKG shows a normal sinus rhythm.
You appear oriented to person and place.
Your pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light.
Pulse is 91 and regular.
Respiration is 13 and non-labored.
Blood pressure is 187 over 122.
Oxygen saturation is now 98%.
You have a history of drinking every day, as reported by your friends. How often and in what amount do you consume alcohol?
On a scale of 1 to 10, with 10 being the worst pain you've ever felt, your pain level is a 10.
Peripheral pulses are intact in all extremities.
You scored 14 on the GCS-oriented to person and place, but not to time or situation.
We performed a BEAST exam and found no signs of stroke.
We recommend you go to [COMPANY_NAME] for further evaluation. Are you comfortable with that?
I will help you stand and pivot to the stretcher. You'll be secured with five restraints and kept warm with a blanket during transport.
Blood pressure is now 188 over 128.
There is no history of allergies affecting medications or surgeries, except for seasonal allergies.
There is noticeable swelling and a small 1-2mm laceration on your right lip and cheek.
Glucose was unmeasurable after the initial reading.
No focal neurological or motor deficits were observed.
You've confirmed [COMPANY_NAME] as your choice of hospital.
You're loaded and secured. We'll update the hospital en route.
I will keep the cold pack and ECG monitoring in place during transport.
There was no swelling or deformity noted in your face or spine.
Based on your breath, there is a noticeable scent of ethanol. It might affect our ability to get your full story.
Did you lose consciousness at all before we arrived?
I don't think so, but I'm not sure.
From your responses, you appear alert and oriented to at least person and place.
Blood pressure is now 180 over 116 with a pulse 91 and respiration 15.
You've confirmed [COMPANY_NAME] as your choice of hospital.
You're being placed on the stretcher now while being monitored for any changes.
You have fully open eyes and respond appropriately to verbal stimuli.
There is no active drainage or obstruction from your nasal passages.
We found no signs of abnormal eye movement or neurological deficits.
Continuous heart rhythm is normal sinus at all time points.
Oxygen remains stable at 97-98%.
You are not refusing IV, but we will avoid placing one per your choice.
No signs of foreign bodies in oral or nasal cavities.
Pulse remains regular and within acceptable range.
You show full movement and do not report pain or numbness.
You clearly cannot remember what caused the punch. Your friends have reported no details.
You've shown satisfactory alertness and verbal response without any signs of confusion.
Glucose was not retested but baseline suggests stable readings with no concerning symptoms.
Pulse is at 95, respiration at 20, and blood pressure remains elevated at 188/128 yet stable.
No obvious head injury signs on this assessment, and you did not complain of headache changes.
Patient is stable with face trauma, intoxication, and neurological findings consistent with AAOx2/4.
You're securely placed with monitoring continuing during transport.
You haven't provided detailed intake, but your alcohol consumption is daily according to friends.
You don't have bruising or obvious lacerations to the upper or lower extremities.
No difficulty with swallowing or cyanosis is reported or observed.
Your medical history is limited to non-insulin diabetes, consistent with no complications reported.
No signs of nuchal rigidity were observed or reported.
You are ready for transport and will be placed on the stretcher safely.
Hospital has been pre-informed of patient condition and needs.
No changes noticed in mental status or breathing during transport.
A BEAST exam was performed with negative results.
Friends state they did not witness the assault and cannot provide further information.
You are able to move all extremities against gravity, implying no acute neurological deficits.
No tooth dislodgment or blood noted aside from the lip injury.
You've acknowledged enforcement staff and were not in acute distress upon our initial contact.
End of transport vitals are stable and consistent.
All set for [COMPANY_NAME].
Final report delivered to receiving RN, with transfers to be made with assistance.
Your mental status remains AAOx2/4 with no known changes.
No stroke signs, spinal injury signs, or spontaneous movements reported throughout care.
Your alertness is sufficient for handling on stretcher and during transport.
Strength is equal bilaterally in all extremities, with no evidence of uneven coordination or loss of power.
